tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows elevated creatinine, proteinuria, and abnormal urine findings (cloudy, brown, low specific gravity), suggesting possible renal impairment or nephropathy. LDL cholesterol is mildly elevated, increasing cardiovascular risk. HbA1c is unexpectedly low despite urine glucose presence, which may indicate glycosuria unrelated to hyperglycemia (e.g., renal glycosuria). Recommend further renal evaluation and correlation with clinical findings.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
Nonproliferative retinopathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Retinopathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
